mnemonics.title: CNF Conversion Steps
mnemonics.content: I.N.S.K.D.R - Implications, Negations, Standardize, Kill (Skolemize), Drop universal, R(epread) to CNF

mnemonics.title: Resolution Basics
mnemonics.content: RESOLVE: REmoce complementary pair, SOLve by unification, VERify result - combine remaining literals

mnemonics.title: MGU Properties
mnemonics.content: MGU is Unique up to renaming - Think of it as the 'cleanest' substitution with no extra baggage

acronyms.usage: Formula expressed as AND of ORs (clauses)
The most general substitution making two literals identical
Set of clauses representing facts and rules

visualTips.visualization: Think of two trains on parallel tracks - each clause is a train. Complementary literals are matching cars going opposite directions. When they collide (resolve), they disappear and remaining cars join into a new train (resolvent).
Like solving a puzzle where pieces (variables and constants) must fit perfectly. MGU is finding the simplest way to make pieces match without forcing them.
Building a case against yourself - assume the opposite (negate theorem), pile on evidence until you find a contradiction (empty clause), proving your original claim.
Like a arrow pointing right: (P1 ∧ P2) → Q. At most one positive literal makes it a simple if-then rule, perfect for Prolog's backward chaining.

analogies.analogy: Resolution is like detective work - you look for contradictions (complementary literals) to eliminate possibilities. Each resolution step narrows down the search space until you find the contradiction (empty clause) that proves guilt or innocence.
Unification is like finding the right key for a lock - you need to find the exact substitution that makes the predicate patterns match perfectly.
Proving by contradiction is like proving someone wrong by making them agree to everything except your point, then showing their position leads to absurdity.
Skolemization is like assigning anonymous witnesses - instead of saying 'someone knows', we create 'witness1' who knows, removing the existential but potentially losing some information.
